steps.title: Identify the student's goal
steps.content: The student wants to specify the average length of the green iguana. The relevant note is: “It has an average length of 4.8 feet.” The correct sentence must state that figure explicitly — not habitat, not diet, not a combination that omits the length.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — The green iguana can be found in Central America.: Gives habitat information; says nothing about length. ✗
  • Choice B — The green iguana has an average length of 4.8 feet.: Directly states the average length of 4.8 feet, fulfilling the goal exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — One species of reptile found in Brazil primarily eats leaves and fruit.: Describes diet and location; omits length entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — The green iguana is a reptile that primarily eats leaves and fruit.: Gives classification and diet; omits length entirely. ✗
B is correct.
